name: Peña Trebivca Return
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Viana Do Bolo navigate a landscape characterized by significant elevation changes, including mountain passes and hills. The region features a mix of forested areas and open terrain, with routes often circling reservoirs. Road cyclists can expect challenging climbs and descents, providing varied riding experiences. The area's topography makes it suitable for those seeking demanding road cycling routes. Best road cycling routes around Viana Do Bolo The most popular road cycling route is Itinerario de Monterrei and Alto do Fojo Loop, a 57.0 miles (91.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 49 minutes to complete. This difficult route features over 1600 meters of elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is As Portas Reservoir Loop, a difficult 30.9 miles (49.7 km) path. This route offers views of the reservoir and surrounding hills. Local road cyclists also love the Peña Trebivca Return, a 48.6 miles (78.2 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 21 minutes. Road cycling around Viana Do Bolo is defined by challenging climbs, mountain passes, and reservoir views. The network offers options primarily for advanced road cyclists, with some moderate routes available. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 60 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Viana Do Bolo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Viana Do Bolo?
A: Road cycling routes in Viana Do Bolo are characterized by significant elevation changes, including mountain passes and hills. You'll encounter a mix of forested areas and open terrain, with many routes circling reservoirs. Expect challenging climbs and descents, providing a varied and demanding riding experience.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Viana Do Bolo?
A: The Viana Do Bolo area primarily offers routes for advanced road cyclists due to its challenging topography. However, there are a couple of moderate routes available, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portela da Canda Pass loop from A Gudiña&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 39.3 km with 639 meters of elevation gain. Most routes feature substantial climbs.

Q: Which road cycling routes offer the most significant climbs in Viana Do Bolo?
A: For those seeking substantial climbs, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936582038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Trebivca Return&lt;/a&gt; stand out, featuring over 1700 meters of elevation gain over 78.2 km. Another challenging option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936491294" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Itinerario de Monterrei and Alto do Fojo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, with more than 1600 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Viana Do Bolo?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Viana Do Bolo are designed as loops. Popular circular routes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936491294" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Itinerario de Monterrei and Alto do Fojo Loop&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936447410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Portas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, both offering scenic circuits through the region's diverse landscape.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: The region offers several scenic spots. You can find stunning views of the Puente Porto reservoir or the Beo Reservoir and Viana de Bolo from the San Cibrao viewpoint. The routes also pass by natural features like the Bibei River Valley and Pías Reservoir. For specific viewpoints, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7989709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Puente Porto reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Beo Reservoir and Viana de Bolo from the San Cibrao viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see while road cycling in Viana Do Bolo?
A: Yes, some routes may bring you close to natural water features. Notably, there is a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall at Embalse de Valdesirgas&lt;/a&gt;, which could be an interesting stop if your route passes nearby.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I explore near the cycling routes?
A: While cycling, you might encounter significant landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of La Tuiza&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6309808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto da Canda (Camino de Santiago)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5458313" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portela da Canda Pass&lt;/a&gt;, which hold historical significance as part of ancient routes.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Viana Do Bolo area?
A: There are over a dozen road cycling routes documented for the Viana Do Bolo area on komoot, offering various distances and difficulty levels, predominantly for experienced riders.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Viana Do Bolo?
A: Road cyclists frequently praise the challenging nature of the routes, the significant elevation gains, and the rewarding views of reservoirs and mountainous terrain. The region's diverse landscape, from forested areas to open hills, also contributes to a memorable riding experience.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through or near towns for amenities?
A: Routes often connect or pass near smaller towns and villages, providing opportunities for amenities. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14936068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chaira das Lamas Viewpoint – A Pobra de Trives Town Center loop from O Barrio dos Xitanos&lt;/a&gt; specifically mentions passing through a town center, which would offer places for refreshments.

Q: What is the typical duration for road cycling routes in Viana Do Bolo?
A: Due to the challenging terrain and significant distances, many routes require several hours to complete. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936491294" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Itinerario de Monterrei and Alto do Fojo Loop&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 3 hours 49 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936582038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Trebivca Return&lt;/a&gt; can take over 5 hours.
tips: Near Alto da Vesada, at an altitude of 1,190m, you will find a magnificent viewpoint towards the valley of the Xares River and the mountains of the Peña Trevinca massif, whose peaks reach the highest levels in Galicia.
At 2127m Pena Trivinca is the highest peak in Galicia. Located to the east of the province of Ourense in the region of Valdeorras
View of the San Sebastián reservoir
